reforms.description: The government is reviewing the R&D tax incentive scheme to increase flexibility and reduce bureaucracy, aiming to improve communication of R&D tax incentives to firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining the R&D tax incentive scheme is expected to enhance its effectiveness, encouraging more firms to engage in R&D activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is currently reviewing the tax incentive scheme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the existing R&D tax incentives, which could lead to increased business investment in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government is currently reviewing the tax incentive scheme.

reforms.description: Lithuania has introduced a dedicated R&D tax credit for collaborative and subcontracted R&D, offering enhanced tax rates for joint projects with universities and national research institutes, aimed at stimulating innovation and collaboration between sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages firms to engage in R&D activities, fostering innovation and collaboration with research institutions, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit is currently in effect, providing incentives for collaborative R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the tax incentive structure, promoting collaboration between businesses and research institutions, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: Lithuania offers a dedicated R&D tax credit for collaborative/subcontracted R&D where firms benefit from an enhanced credit-tax rate of 30% for joint or contracted R&D with universities.

reforms.description: The Innovation Promotion Fund, operational since January 2021, supports investment in basic and applied research, as well as experimental development and innovation, with funding from government budgets and EU sources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing direct funding for R&D, the fund aims to stimulate innovation and support young firms, thereby enhancing overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been operational since January 2021, supporting various R&D initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The fund represents a significant investment in R&D, aimed at enhancing innovation capabilities across sectors, particularly for young firms.
reforms.source_quote: The Innovation Promotion Fund, in operation since January 2021, supports investment in basic and applied research, as well experimental development and innovation.

reforms.description: The ongoing reform of Lithuania's innovation system aims to consolidate various agencies and support programmes into a single Innovation Agency, enhancing coordination and effectiveness in promoting innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining the innovation support system, the reform is expected to improve the efficiency of resource allocation and enhance the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.status_evidence: The establishment of a single Innovation Agency is scheduled to be in full operation in the course of 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the governance of the innovation system, aiming to enhance coordination and reduce fragmentation, which is critical for effective innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: A core element of the reform is the establishment of a single Innovation Agency, scheduled to be in full operation in the course of 2022.

reforms.description: Lithuania is introducing new national guidelines for developing the fintech sector, which will involve public and private stakeholders to promote digital financial services and technological innovations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The guidelines aim to enhance the fintech sector's growth, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation in financial services.
reforms.status_evidence: The guidelines are being introduced with the participation of stakeholders.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the regulatory framework for the fintech sector, which is crucial for fostering innovation and attracting new market entrants.
reforms.source_quote: Lithuania is introducing new national guidelines for developing the fintech sector, with the participation of public and private stakeholders.

reforms.description: Lithuania is reforming its innovation system to enhance support for business R&D and encourage private investment, aiming to improve overall innovation performance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing support for business R&D, the reform is expected to stimulate private sector investment and drive innovation, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The ongoing reform of the innovation system is a step in the right direction.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the innovation support framework, which is crucial for enhancing Lithuania's competitiveness and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The ongoing reform of the innovation system is a step in the right direction.

reforms.description: The government has started to make the vocational path more attractive by providing school boards with more power and making study programmes more flexible, aiming to improve firm-based learning and vocational education and training (VET).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing vocational education and training will improve the skills of the workforce, aligning them better with market needs and boosting productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has initiated reforms to enhance the vocational education system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to the vocational education system, which is crucial for aligning education with labor market demands.
reforms.source_quote: The government has started to make the vocational path more attractive, by providing school boards with more power and making study programmes more flexible.

reforms.description: The government is developing a digitalisation strategy to enhance investment in innovation and remove barriers to the adoption of advanced technologies by firms, particularly smaller ones.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering digitalisation, the strategy aims to boost productivity and innovation across the economy, particularly benefiting smaller firms that face barriers.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is actively developing a digitalisation strategy to enhance innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is a significant step towards improving the digital landscape and innovation capacity of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government is developing a digitalisation strategy to reap the benefits of new digital technologies and to boost innovation and productivity.

reforms.description: A national investment fund (NPI) was legislated to finance sustainable investments deemed strategically important for Lithuania's economy, consolidating four existing public investment funds to enhance investment strategies and risk management.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The NPI aims to increase leverage for sustainable investments, which can enhance productivity and economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: The NPI was legislated in 2019 and is currently being set up.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the NPI represents a significant structural change in public investment strategy, aiming to consolidate and enhance the effectiveness of public funding for sustainable projects.
reforms.source_quote: A national investment fund (NPI) was legislated in 2019 and is currently being set up. Its purpose is to finance sustainable investment - in both the public and private sector - thought to be strategically important for the Lithuanian economy.

reforms.description: The fintech sector in Lithuania has expanded rapidly, supported by a coordinated strategy from government institutions to enhance competition and innovation in financial services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The growth of the fintech sector enhances competition in financial services, leading to increased innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The fintech sector has become one of the largest in the EU, with significant growth in the number of firms and employees.
reforms.importance_rationale: The coordinated strategy has led to substantial growth in the fintech sector, which is crucial for enhancing competition and innovation in the financial market.
reforms.source_quote: Lithuania’s financial technology sector (fintech) has become one of the largest in the European Union, having grown by 20% annually since 2016.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase direct support for innovation through grants and stronger collaboration with universities and schools, addressing the current imbalance where tax incentives are favored over direct support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing direct support for innovation is expected to enhance the capacity for research and development, fostering a more balanced innovation ecosystem.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has developed plans to increase direct support for innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards enhancing direct support mechanisms for innovation, which could lead to improved outcomes in research and development.
reforms.source_quote: The government should thoroughly assess the effectiveness of tax incentives and consider increasing direct support for innovation, e.g. through grants or stronger collaboration with universities and schools.

reforms.description: Lithuania aims to increase support for business innovation from 0.025% to 0.1% of GDP, addressing the low take-up rate of existing R&D tax incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing funding for business innovation, the reform is expected to stimulate R&D activities and enhance overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to increase support for business innovation from 0.025% to 0.1% of GDP.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the financial support for innovation, which is critical for fostering a competitive business environment.
reforms.source_quote: Increase support for business innovation from 0.025% to 0.1% of GDP.

reforms.description: The government has stepped up financial support for firms that hire apprentices, increasing the subsidy from 40% to 70% of an apprentice’s wage in selected cases, to enhance work-based learning opportunities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing firms to hire apprentices, the reform aims to improve the skills of the workforce and reduce youth unemployment, thereby enhancing productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has increased financial support for firms hiring apprentices.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing vocational training policies, aiming to enhance the relevance of education to labor market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The government has recently stepped-up financial support for firms that hire apprentices, by offering 70% instead of 40% of an apprentice’s wage in selected cases.

reforms.description: The government plans to allocate around 20% of public funding for higher education based on performance targets agreed with institutions, aiming to improve labor market relevance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Linking funding to labor market performance encourages universities to adapt their curricula to meet the demands of the job market, potentially increasing productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to allocate around 20% of funding according to performance targets.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant shift in funding strategy aimed at enhancing the alignment of higher education with labor market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to allocate around 20% of funding according to performance targets agreed with higher education institutions.

reforms.description: Lithuania plans to increase public investment in targeted research and development, particularly in the transport sector and agriculture, to support its ambitious climate targets.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased investment in R&D can lead to innovative solutions that enhance productivity and sustainability in key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken steps towards decarbonisation, including plans for increased R&D investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D in critical sectors, aligning with national climate goals and fostering long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Increase public investment in targeted research and development and green infrastructure.

reforms.description: Lithuania's National Digitisation Development Programme 2021-2030 outlines priorities for digitalising the economy, including the promotion of digital skills and the integration of advanced technologies into public services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital skills and infrastructure is expected to boost productivity and innovation across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme sets out specific plans for digital transformation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This programme represents a comprehensive approach to digital transformation, addressing multiple sectors and aiming for significant economic impact.
reforms.source_quote: The National Digitisation Development Programme 2021-2030 sets out the priorities and guidelines for digitalising the economy.

reforms.description: The Innovation Promotion Fund, operational since 2021, aims to increase overall R&D investment in Lithuania to 1.5% of GDP by 2024, enhancing support for business R&D and collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D investment is expected to drive innovation and productivity growth in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been operational since 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D investment, crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Innovation Promotion Fund, in operation since 2021, is expected to contribute to this end.

reforms.description: The government has introduced new provisions to simplify conditions for non-EU investors to transfer employees to Lithuania, easing regulations that previously required assessment of qualifications by Public Employment Services. This reform aims to attract skilled workers and enhance innovation activity in the country.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By easing the migration of skilled workers, the reform enhances the talent pool available to firms, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The provisions were enacted in 2021 to facilitate employee transfers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the regulatory environment for attracting skilled labor, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: New provisions enacted in 2021 simplified the conditions for investors from non-EU countries to transfer their employees to Lithuania.

reforms.description: The government has initiated a plan to broaden the range of financing sources for SMEs, including the establishment of a Capital Market Council to oversee the implementation of measures aimed at increasing alternative financing options. This includes a focus on crowdfunding and business angel networks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing access to diverse financing options, the reform supports the growth of innovative startups and SMEs, which can lead to increased economic activity and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The action plan aims to increase alternative financing options for Lithuanian firms by 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This comprehensive plan addresses critical financing gaps for SMEs and startups, which are essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Bank of Lithuania has recently announced an action plan that aims to increase significantly by 2025 the alternative financing options for Lithuanian firms.

reforms.description: The Lithuanian government supports the digital transformation of SMEs through the Business Consultant LT programme, which provides consultations and information on new technologies and financing options.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the capacity of SMEs to adopt digital technologies, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is actively providing consultations to SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to enhance the digital capabilities of SMEs, which is crucial for their competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government supports the digital transformation of SMEs in Lithuania mainly through the Business Consultant LT programme, providing business development consultations for SMEs.

reforms.description: The Lithuanian government finances 85% of the costs incurred by eligible micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises for implementing e-commerce models, aiming to boost their digital capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the transition of SMEs to digital platforms, enhancing their market reach and operational efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The government finances 85% of the cost incurred by eligible enterprises for e-commerce implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative significantly lowers the financial barrier for SMEs to adopt e-commerce, which is essential for their growth in a digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: Since 2021, the government finances 85% of the cost incurred by the eligible micro and small and medium-sized enterprises for the implementation of e-commerce models.

reforms.description: The creation of technological Lithuanian language resources for AI solutions is part of Lithuania’s Recovery and Resilience Plan, aimed at enhancing the capacity of smaller firms to utilize digital technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improves access to AI technologies for smaller firms, fostering innovation and competitiveness in the digital economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative is part of the Recovery and Resilience Plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for enhancing the technological capabilities of smaller firms, which is crucial for their adaptation to AI advancements.
reforms.source_quote: The creation of technological Lithuanian language resources, under Lithuania’s Recovery and Resilience Plan, for Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions is expected to increase the capacity of smaller firms to effectively use digital technologies.

reforms.description: The government plans to introduce a revised set of cybersecurity requirements to enhance digital security risk management among firms, particularly in light of rising digital threats.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving cybersecurity measures can enhance trust in digital services, thereby promoting digital transformation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to introduce a revised set of cybersecurity requirements.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical area of digital security, which is essential for the overall digital transformation of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to introduce a revised set of cybersecurity requirements.

reforms.description: The Digital Transformation in Education project aims to enhance the digital skills of educators and improve the use of ICT tools in schools, particularly for students with special needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving educators' digital competencies, the project aims to better prepare students for a digital economy, thus enhancing future productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Digital Transformation in Education project was launched in 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for improving the digital skills of educators, which is crucial for adapting the education system to the demands of a digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Digital Transformation in Education project under way aims to strengthen the use of ICT tools in schools.

reforms.description: The government plans to relate around 20% of public funding to higher education institutions to the achievement of performance targets, including additional funding for degree completions in disciplines important for the labor market, such as digital transformation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Aligning funding with performance targets encourages institutions to adapt their programs to meet labor market needs, thereby enhancing the skills of graduates and supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Ongoing reforms plan to relate public funding to performance targets.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public funding is allocated, aiming to improve the responsiveness of higher education to labor market needs, which is crucial for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: Ongoing reforms plan to relate around 20% of public funding to higher education institutions to the achievement of the performance targets agreed with such institutions.

reforms.description: New legislation introduces short-cycle tertiary programmes in fields such as computer engineering and programme systems, aimed at improving pathways for vocational education graduates into higher education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By creating more accessible pathways into higher education, this reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, particularly in high-demand fields like ICT, thus supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: New 2022 legislation introduces short-cycle tertiary programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it expands educational opportunities for vocational students, addressing skill shortages in the labor market.
reforms.source_quote: New 2022 legislation introduces short-cycle tertiary programmes in the fields of computer engineering, programme systems and tourism.

reforms.description: The government plans to introduce a training levy on employers to finance the establishment of training funds, aimed at increasing training opportunities for workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing firms to invest in employee training, the reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby improving productivity and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The option of introducing a training levy on employers could be considered.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant policy shift aimed at enhancing workforce skills, which is crucial for economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The option of introducing a training levy on employers... that can finance the establishment of training funds could be considered.

reforms.description: The government aims to develop a national lifelong learning platform to serve as a comprehensive resource for adult education, enhancing participation in training programs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing a centralized resource for adult education, the platform is expected to increase participation in training, thereby improving the skill set of the workforce and supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Proceed with the development of national lifelong learning platform that will serve as a 'one-stop shop' for adult education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to address the low participation in adult learning, which is critical for enhancing the skills of the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: Proceed with the development of national lifelong learning platform that will serve as a 'one-stop shop' for adult education.
